features.name: App-connections hardening & merge
features.description: Land in-flight persistent-login feature through gates; retrofit ADR-02; fix engine↔app-connections erosion
features.valueWhy: Lands the in-flight persistent-login feature safely and clears the biggest index.ts contention before Wave 1.

features.name: Schema contracts
features.description: Template declares output JSON Schema; validated each run
features.valueWhy: Templates without a declared shape are unverifiable; schema contracts make drift and self-healing possible.

features.name: Drift detection
features.description: Diff live shape vs contract → flag; expose reusable diff primitive
features.valueWhy: Sites change silently; drift detection is the reliability trigger every self-heal/monitor feature reuses.
features.toolOrSurface: reusable diff primitive + drift flag on run result

features.name: Nightly re-verification + badges
features.valueWhy: Community trust needs proof templates still work; nightly badges make verification visible and continuous.

features.name: Self-healing templates
features.description: On drift, hand forensic DOM+old script to the calling agent → fix → verify → registry PR (never auto-merge)
features.valueWhy: Self-healing is the moat vs. computer-use: fix once, stay fixed automatically instead of degrading silently.
features.toolOrSurface: self-heal.mjs forensic handoff + registry PR helper

features.name: synthesize_schema (agent-native)
features.description: renderPage() → agent writes script → dry-run via executeExtraction → register
features.valueWhy: Lets any calling agent author a new template without a human writing Playwright by hand.

features.name: Computer-use crystallization
features.description: Agent solves unmapped site via computer-use → record → crystallize → optional auto-PR
features.valueWhy: Extends authoring to sites with no existing template at all via computer-use, then crystallizes to deterministic.

features.name: Template pipelines / DAG
features.valueWhy: Real workflows chain multiple templates; a pipeline runner turns single calls into composable capability.

features.name: CEL conditional branching
features.description: Port Kriya's CEL evaluator; action-sequence branches on live state
features.valueWhy: Live-state branching (found item / no results / captcha) is required for realistic multi-step flows.

features.name: Bidirectional flows
features.description: Read A → transform → form-fill write into B
features.valueWhy: Read+write flows (copy data from A into B) unlock RPA-replacement use cases, not just read-only scraping.

features.name: Transform / normalize layer
features.valueWhy: Raw extracted shapes rarely match what a consumer wants; jq-like mapping normalizes output once, centrally.

features.name: Signed provenance receipts
features.valueWhy: Regulated/financial consumers need tamper-evident proof of what was fetched and when.

features.name: Policy engine
features.valueWhy: Respecting robots.txt/ToS and rate limits is the ethical/legal floor for a scraping-adjacent product.

features.name: Encrypted credential vault
features.description: Encrypted secrets injected at run; distinct from app-connections
features.valueWhy: Some templates need real secrets (API keys, form values) distinct from browser login state.

features.name: Metrics 2.0 (SLA)
features.valueWhy: Success-rate + latency SLAs are what a paying/consumer audience actually judges reliability by.

features.name: static-http template kind
features.description: cheerio fast-path (no browser) — 10–50x faster for no-JS pages
features.valueWhy: Most pages need no browser at all; a cheerio fast-path is 10-50x cheaper and enables cloud-tier free execution.

features.name: Short-TTL result cache
features.valueWhy: Repeated identical calls in a short window shouldn't re-run a full browser session.

features.name: OpenTelemetry observability
features.valueWhy: Standard OTel export lets ops teams plug APImeMCP into their existing observability stack.

features.name: Ephemeral hosted endpoint
features.description: read-registry + zero-state HTTP endpoint; the substrate X01/X02 productize
features.valueWhy: A zero-state hosted endpoint is the substrate the entire cloud/consumer platform (X01/X02) is built on.

features.name: Close items 4/5 gaps
features.description: apimemcp add CLI + lint-in-CI + live network-behavior check in verify-registry
features.valueWhy: Closes known roadmap gaps (CLI ergonomics, CI lint, live network check) blocking a clean v1 release.
features.toolOrSurface: apimemcp add CLI + CI lint + live network check

features.name: Change-monitoring mesh
features.description: Diff template results over time → events (reuses F02 diff)
features.valueWhy: Turns one-shot diffing into a standing mesh of watched templates — the basis of the mobile monitor feature.

features.name: NL→template one-shot
features.description: 'make me an API for X' wrapper (F05+F06)
features.valueWhy: Removes the last friction step: an agent just says what it wants and gets a working template.

features.name: Semantic template discovery
features.description: Search 'what can APImeMCP do for domain X' (local+registry)
features.valueWhy: Discoverability turns the registry from a list into an answerable "what can this do for me" surface.

features.name: Golden-snapshot regression
features.valueWhy: Regression protection so a template fix or engine change can't silently break output shape again.

features.name: Marketplace reputation + semver
features.valueWhy: Reputation + semver make the registry trustworthy at scale as more contributors show up.

features.name: OpenAPI + client export
features.description: Generate OpenAPI + typed client from a bundle
features.valueWhy: Standard OpenAPI/client export lets any existing tool integrate with zero APImeMCP-specific glue.

features.name: Platform monorepo scaffold
features.description: Turborepo (apps/web, apps/mobile, packages/shared) + Vercel project + CI/CD
features.valueWhy: One monorepo with shared types is what makes web+mobile+cloud a coherent platform instead of three silos.

features.name: Cross-surface design system
features.description: Shared tokens; phosphor/void identity elevated; web+mobile
features.valueWhy: A single design system keeps web and mobile visually and behaviorally one product.

features.name: Registry browser + search
features.valueWhy: The registry has to be browsable before anyone can discover or run a community template.

features.name: Template detail + schema/docs
features.description: Rendered docs + F01 schema + Run entry
features.valueWhy: Consumers need to see the schema/verification/provenance for a template before trusting a Run.

features.name: Web run console
features.valueWhy: The core consumer loop — run a community API and see results — happens here.

features.name: Contribute flow
features.valueWhy: Lowers the bar for new contributors, growing registry coverage (the flywheel).

features.name: Auth + accounts + dashboard
features.valueWhy: Accounts are required before monitors/history/API keys can exist at all.

features.name: Landing + interactive hero
features.description: Vision pages + live compile-and-run hero demo
features.valueWhy: First impression and conversion surface; the live hero demo proves the product in 10 seconds.

features.name: Execution API gateway
features.valueWhy: The single gateway every web/mobile run call goes through; auth + rate-limit live here once.

features.name: Safe registry-only runtime
features.valueWhy: Phones/browsers can't run Playwright — this sandboxed runtime is the only way they run templates at all.

features.name: Durable jobs + heavy fallback
features.valueWhy: Multi-step/monitor flows exceed a single request's time budget; durable orchestration is required.

features.name: Results delivery
features.valueWhy: Results have to reach the user live (web) or later (mobile push) without the site staying open.

features.name: Monitors service
features.valueWhy: Push-on-change monitors are the single biggest mobile consumer hook (price drop / restock / new filing).

features.name: Encrypted cookies + vault
features.valueWhy: User-supplied cookies are sensitive; encrypted transit + opt-in vault is the trust floor for auth templates.

features.name: Registry mirror/cache DB
features.description: Postgres mirror + sync for fast catalog
features.valueWhy: A fast Postgres mirror avoids hitting the git registry on every browse/search request.

features.name: Expo app scaffold
features.description: iOS+Android, Expo Router, EAS, Clerk auth
features.valueWhy: The native app shell every mobile screen and EAS build depends on.

features.name: Mobile design system impl
features.valueWhy: A native-feeling (not webview) app is required for store approval and consumer trust.

features.name: Browse/registry screens
features.valueWhy: Mobile users need to find a template before they can run or monitor it.

features.name: Run screen + result views
features.valueWhy: "Run a community API from your phone" is the platform's headline consumer capability.

features.name: Monitors + push
features.valueWhy: Push-on-change monitors delivered to the phone — the consumer wedge described in the vision.

features.name: Run history + account + cookies
features.valueWhy: Users need history + a safe place for their own cookies/credentials on-device.

features.name: App-store prep
features.valueWhy: Required before either app store will accept a public listing.
